title: Commercial Status
description: Machine-readable payment contract for AI agent tool access. An agent reads this to discover cost, send payment, and prove it paid — all without a human in the loop.

status.description: High-level commercial tier. 'free' means no payment config is needed. All other statuses should include pricing and payment_config.

pricing.description: What the tool costs and how it's measured.

model.description: How usage is metered. per_call is simplest for agents.

amount.description: Cost per unit (call, token, byte, etc.) in the currency specified.

currency.description: Payment currency. USDC on Base is the default — stable, cheap, fast.

free_tier.description: Free allowance before payment kicks in. An agent checks this first. Tools enforce free tier limits per agent_id (from the X-OpenTrust-Agent-Identity header) — agent_id is the stable cross-session identifier. session_id resets per task and must not be used for free tier tracking. A tool that receives calls without an agent identity header may fall back to IP-based limiting, but should document this in pricing.notes.

identity_required.description: If true, the tool requires a valid X-OpenTrust-Agent-Identity header to track free tier usage. Calls without identity are rejected or billed immediately. Recommended for any tool with a free_tier to prevent quota exhaustion by anonymous callers.

tiers_model.description: Required when pricing.model is 'tiered'. graduated: each additional unit is priced at its tier rate — cost decreases as volume grows (like AWS data transfer). flat_per_tier: all units within a band pay the same rate — once you cross a threshold, every unit in that band is billed at that band's rate. Agents use this to predict costs accurately before committing.

tiers.description: For tiered pricing — list of volume tiers with per-unit cost. Tiers must be ordered ascending by up_to. The final tier must have up_to: null (unlimited). See tiers_model for how costs are calculated across tier boundaries.

up_to.description: Upper bound for this tier (inclusive). null means unlimited — must be the last tier.

amount.description: Per-unit cost for this tier in the declared currency.

payment_config.description: Where and how to send payment. An agent reads this to construct and submit a transaction.

type.description: crypto_direct: agent sends USDC directly to a wallet address. coinbase_commerce: agent calls a Coinbase Commerce checkout URL. payment_gateway: agent calls a custom endpoint that returns a payment intent.

network.description: Which chain to send payment on. Base (Coinbase L2) is the default — sub-cent fees, fast finality.

wallet_address.description: For crypto_direct: the wallet address that receives payment. Agent sends amount_usdc here.

checkout_url.description: For coinbase_commerce: the Coinbase Commerce checkout or charge creation endpoint.

gateway_url.description: For payment_gateway: endpoint the agent POSTs to get a payment intent. Returns a payment_intent_id and destination.

supported_tokens.description: Which tokens this tool accepts. Agents should prefer USDC.

escrow_config.description: Optional escrow support for this tool. If present, agents can use escrow-protected payments.

access_config.description: How an agent proves it paid and gets access. This is the piece that makes agent-to-agent payments work without a human in the loop.

type.description: transaction_proof: agent includes txHash in each tool call header — tool verifies on-chain. webhook_token: tool verifies payment via webhook and returns a short-lived JWT. api_key_issued: tool issues a reusable API key after payment confirmation. open_after_payment: tool is gated but unlocks for the session after any payment is confirmed.

verification_endpoint.description: For webhook_token and api_key_issued: the endpoint the agent calls to exchange a transaction hash for a token or key.

header_name.description: For transaction_proof: the HTTP header the agent includes the txHash in (e.g. 'X-Payment-TxHash').

token_ttl_seconds.description: For webhook_token: how long the returned token is valid.

token_scope.description: What the token grants access to (e.g. 'single_call', 'session', 'monthly').
